skills.name: deep-research
skills.desc: Thorough source-heavy research brief with citations. Triggers on "deep research", "investigate", "research brief".

skills.name: feynman-bridge
skills.desc: Invoke the feynman CLI for deep academic and long-horizon research. Use once per research task before synthesising findings.

skills.name: source-comparison
skills.desc: Grounded comparison matrix across tools, papers, frameworks, or approaches.

skills.name: literature-review
skills.desc: Comprehensive academic literature review with synthesis and consensus mapping.

skills.name: novelty-check
skills.desc: Verify whether a research idea or approach is genuinely novel against recent literature.

skills.name: peer-review
skills.desc: Simulate tough peer review of any research artifact — paper, draft, proposal, report.

skills.name: idea-refine
skills.desc: Refine ideas through structured divergent and convergent thinking.

skills.name: eli5
skills.desc: Explain research papers or technical concepts in plain English with concrete analogies.

skills.name: alpha-research

skills.name: research-wiki
skills.desc: Persistent research knowledge base. Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern — compile once, keep current.

skills.name: research-refine
skills.desc: Turn a vague research direction into a concrete, problem-anchored implementation-ready plan.

skills.name: media-extract
skills.desc: Universal media extraction and analysis — YouTube, web articles, local video/audio/PDF, pasted transcripts. Extracts summaries, golden nuggets, workflows, commands, chapters, quotes. Cleans meeting transcripts. Uses yt-dlp + Gemini visual analysis.

skills.name: defuddle
skills.desc: Extract clean markdown from web pages using the Defuddle CLI, removing clutter.

skills.name: jobs
skills.desc: Inspect active background research — running processes, scheduled tasks, pending missions.

skills.name: watch
skills.desc: Set up a recurring research watch on a topic, company, or paper area.

skills.name: pdf

skills.name: xlsx
skills.desc: Open, read, edit, or fix spreadsheet files.

skills.name: pptx
skills.desc: Create, read, or edit PowerPoint presentation files.

skills.name: file-intel
skills.desc: Run Gemini file processor on any folder. Extracts content from PDF, PPTX, XLSX, DOCX, CSV, JSON and generates Obsidian-ready summaries.

skills.name: preview
skills.desc: Preview or export Markdown, LaTeX, or research artifacts as rendered documents or PDFs.

skills.name: spec-driven-development
skills.desc: Write a spec before coding starts. Use when requirements are unclear or unstated.

skills.name: planning-and-task-breakdown
skills.desc: Break a spec into atomic, implementable tasks. Use before writing any code.

skills.name: api-and-interface-design
skills.desc: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing REST endpoints or module boundaries.

skills.name: documentation-and-adrs
skills.desc: Write ADRs and project docs. Use when making architectural decisions or shipping features.

skills.name: verification-before-completion
skills.desc: Evidence before assertions. Run before any completion claim, commit, or PR.

skills.name: systematic-debugging
skills.desc: Root-cause before fixes. Use when encountering any bug or unexpected behavior.

skills.name: requesting-code-review
skills.desc: Dispatch a code reviewer subagent. Use after completing a task or major feature.

skills.name: receiving-code-review
skills.desc: Receive and evaluate code review feedback with technical rigor, not performative agreement.

skills.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skills.desc: Guide completion of development work — verify tests, merge options, cleanup.

skills.name: mcp-builder
skills.desc: Scaffold and build MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. Use when building new MCP integrations.

skills.name: anthropic-claude-api
skills.desc: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Handles prompt caching, model migrations, tool use, batch, and citations. TRIGGER on code importing anthropic/@anthropic-ai/sdk or any Claude API feature question.

skills.name: agent-sandboxes
skills.desc: Operate E2B agent sandboxes via CLI. Use when running code in isolation, testing packages, or executing commands safely in a sandbox environment.

skills.name: audit-python
skills.desc: Audit and optimize Python codebases. Quality review, structure analysis, and improvement recommendations.

skills.name: audit-typescript
skills.desc: Audit and optimize TypeScript codebases. Quality review, structure analysis, and improvement recommendations.

skills.name: bounded-shell
skills.desc: Context-preserving shell execution. Caps long command output and auto-summarizes when output would exceed token budget.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.desc: Test local web applications using Playwright. Verify frontend functionality and debug UI.

skills.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
skills.desc: Test and debug browser-based apps using real browsers and DevTools. Use when inspecting DOM, capturing console errors, or verifying frontend behaviour.

skills.name: ci-cd-and-automation
skills.desc: Set up or modify CI/CD pipelines. Use when automating quality gates, configuring builds, or wiring deployment workflows.

skills.name: code-review-and-quality
skills.desc: Multi-axis code review across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use before merging any change.

skills.name: code-simplification
skills.desc: Refactor code for clarity without changing behaviour. Use when code works but is hard to read, maintain, or extend.

skills.name: context-engineering
skills.desc: Optimize agent context setup. Use when starting a session, when output quality degrades, or when switching tasks.

skills.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
skills.desc: Systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, or behaviour doesn't match expectations — before proposing fixes.

skills.name: deprecation-and-migration
skills.desc: Manage removal of old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another or retiring legacy code.

skills.name: frontend-ui-engineering
skills.desc: Build production-quality UIs. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or handling accessibility.

skills.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
skills.desc: Structure git workflow — committing, branching, resolving conflicts, PRs. Use on any code change.

skills.name: incremental-implementation
skills.desc: Deliver changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature touching more than one file — ship in small, reviewable slices.

skills.name: performance-optimization
skills.desc: Optimize application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, regressions are suspected, or load targets need to be hit.

skills.name: security-and-hardening
skills.desc: Harden code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, auth, data storage, or external integrations.

skills.name: shipping-and-launch
skills.desc: Prepare production launches. Use when deploying to production, building a pre-launch checklist, or setting up monitoring and rollback.

skills.name: source-driven-development
skills.desc: Ground every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when you need authoritative, source-cited code free of outdated patterns.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.desc: Drive development with tests. Use when implementing logic, fixing bugs, or changing behaviour — tests first, code second.

skills.name: using-agent-skills
skills.desc: Discover and invoke agent skills. Use at session start or when determining which skill applies to the current task.

skills.name: gemini-interactions-api
skills.desc: Call the Gemini API for text generation, chat, streaming, and function calling.

skills.name: gemini-live-api-dev
skills.desc: Build real-time bidirectional streaming apps with the Gemini Live API.

skills.name: vertex-ai-api-dev
skills.desc: Use Gemini on Google Cloud Vertex AI. Enterprise environments, Gen AI SDK.

skills.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skills.desc: Dispatch independent tasks to parallel agents with isolated context windows.

skills.name: subagent-driven-development
skills.desc: Execute implementation plans by dispatching fresh subagents per task with two-stage review.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.desc: Explore intent and requirements before any implementation. HARD GATE before building.

skills.name: writing-plans
skills.desc: Write comprehensive implementation plans before touching code.

skills.name: writing-skills
skills.desc: TDD for skill creation — RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle using pressure scenarios and subagent testing.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.desc: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance.

skills.name: skill-optimizer
skills.desc: Karpathy autoresearch loop for autonomously improving Claude Code skill files.

skills.name: meta-optimize
skills.desc: Analyze skill invocation logs and propose improvements to skills and workflow sequences.

skills.name: autoresearch
skills.desc: Autonomous experiment loop — try ideas, measure results, keep what works, repeat.

skills.name: library
skills.desc: Private skill distribution system. Install, push, remove, sync, list, or search skills from the P&A catalog. Triggers on /library commands or skill distribution tasks.

skills.name: agent-session-summary
skills.desc: Structured end-of-session handoff. Saves to ~/dev/projects/rnd/deepagents/sessions/. Use /wrap.

skills.name: session-log
skills.desc: Write a durable session log capturing completed work, findings, open questions, next steps.

skills.name: session-search
skills.desc: Search past session transcripts and logs to recover prior work and decisions.

skills.name: tldr
skills.desc: Save a summary of this conversation to the vault. Key decisions, things to remember, next actions. Stored in the right vault folder automatically.

skills.desc: Interact with Obsidian vaults — read, create, search, manage notes and tasks.

skills.name: obsidian-markdown
skills.desc: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, callouts, and properties.

skills.name: obsidian-bases
skills.desc: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) — database-like views of notes.

skills.name: daily
skills.desc: Start the day with vault context. Read or create today's daily note, surface top priorities.

skills.name: vault-setup
skills.desc: Interactive Obsidian vault configurator. Builds personalized vault structure, CLAUDE.md, and slash commands.

skills.name: json-canvas
skills.desc: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, and groups. Use for visual canvases and mind maps in Obsidian.

skills.name: paper-writing
skills.desc: Turn research findings into a polished paper-style draft with sections and citations.

skills.name: paper-code-audit
skills.desc: Compare a paper's claims against its public codebase. Identify mismatches and gaps.

skills.name: replication
skills.desc: Plan or execute a replication of a paper's results, claims, or benchmarks.

skills.name: idea-discovery
skills.desc: Full idea discovery pipeline — survey field, generate ideas, check novelty, refine.

skills.name: auto-review-loop
skills.desc: Autonomous multi-round review and improvement loop until quality threshold is met.

skills.name: saas-decommission
skills.desc: Audits a prospect's or client's SaaS stack to find consolidation, AI-replacement, and license-reclamation opportunities. Produces a CFO-ready savings memo with confidence bands across four lenses: license reclamation, tool consolidation, AI replacement, and strategic consolidation. ALWAYS use this skill when a prospect is exploring AI consulting, asking what AI could do, raising budget objections, or mentioning specific SaaS tools by name. Fire on the very first qualifying call — the P&A opener. Wedge that pays for itself before scope is signed.

skills.name: problem-decomposition
skills.desc: Decomposes a client problem into a MECE issue tree using the 5 Ways to MECE framework (opposite words, math equations, segmentation, process steps, conceptual frameworks). Validates the tree, identifies which branches to investigate first. ALWAYS use this skill before designing any AI solution, before scoping any engagement, before writing agent architecture, when a problem feels too big or fuzzy, or whenever you see "we want AI to help with X" or "figure out Z". This is the spine of every Principal engagement. Skipping produces solutions that fix symptoms, not root causes.

skills.name: agentic-workflow-solutioning
skills.desc: Designs the actual AI agent or workflow architecture for a defined problem. Chooses between single LLM call, workflow, tool-use workflow, open-ended agent, multi-agent, or skill pack. Specifies all 6 guardrails (identity/access, data scope, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, kill conditions, cost ceilings, observability) and provides a Mermaid topology diagram with technology table. ALWAYS use this skill after problem-decomposition has constrained the problem, BEFORE writing any code or architecture diagram, when asked "how should we build this", what architecture, one agent or many, or about tools, MCP, skills, or deployment. This is where Principal's taste — earned from production agents — is encoded.

skills.name: threat-model
skills.desc: Produces a STRIDE-style threat model adapted for AI agents. Identifies prompt injection (direct, indirect, second-order), tool abuse, data exfiltration, supply chain threats, identity confusion, and runaway autonomous behavior. Scores threats by likelihood x impact, recommends mitigations, produces residual risk register client signs off on. ALWAYS use this skill before any agent or AI workflow goes to build, before any client-facing deployment, when architecture changes (new tool, new MCP, new data source), after agentic-workflow-solutioning produces spec, when deployment touches PII, financial, customer, or regulated data, or whenever conversation includes "deploy", "production", or "go live". Skipping this skill is malpractice.

skills.name: engagement-retrospective
skills.desc: Runs a structured 6-part post-engagement retrospective and writes outputs to the P&A case base. Captures the story, job-to-be-done outcome, problem shape, what worked, what didn't, and what is reusable across future engagements. ALWAYS use this skill at close of every Principal engagement, before final invoicing, before follow-on scoping, or at any natural milestone where lessons should be captured. The compounding loop — every retro makes the next engagement faster and more defensible. Skipping means engagement N+1 starts from blank page.

skills.name: pna-publish
skills.desc: Publish any artifact to the P&A sessions archive at pna-sessions.vercel.app. Use when archiving sessions, diagrams, or research outputs.

skills.name: publish-research
skills.desc: Publish research results to pna-sessions as formatted pages. Runs comparative analysis across results and notifies Alex.

skills.name: cerberus
skills.desc: Deep client research and architecture validation for Principal & Agent engagements.

skills.name: doc-coauthoring
skills.desc: Structured workflow for co-authoring documentation, proposals, and technical specs.

skills.name: write
skills.desc: Generalized writing skill for any platform and format — newsletters, YouTube scripts, guides, sales pages, social posts. Handles structure, drafting, and platform conventions.

skills.name: voice
skills.desc: Apply an authentic writing voice to produced content. Personality layer invoked by other writing skills or directly.

skills.name: research-creator
skills.desc: Topic and competitor research for any content type. Identifies content gaps and competitive landscape before writing.

skills.name: title
skills.desc: Generate optimized titles, headlines, and subject lines for any content type. YouTube, newsletters, blog posts, social.

skills.name: hook
skills.desc: Create retention-optimized opening hooks — video intros, newsletter leads, social post openers.

skills.name: extract-ideas

skills.name: thumbnail

skills.name: social-graphic
skills.desc: Create social media graphics, newsletter headers, blog feature images, OG images, profile banners.

skills.name: design-system
skills.desc: Create, update, or refresh a visual design system or art style guide.

skills.name: brand-guidelines

skills.name: nanobanana
skills.desc: AI image generation and editing using Google Gemini models (nano banana). Requires GEMINI_API_KEY.

skills.name: plan-newsletter
skills.desc: Plan a complete newsletter issue — research, draft, subject line, opening hook, and social promotion posts.

skills.name: optimize-newsletter
skills.desc: Optimize a newsletter draft or write a full issue from an outline. Polish, improve, rewrite.

skills.name: newsletter-visuals

skills.name: newsletter-to-video
skills.desc: Repurpose a newsletter issue into a YouTube video outline.

skills.name: plan-video
skills.desc: Complete YouTube video plan — title options, thumbnail concepts, hooks, content outline.

skills.name: repurpose-video
skills.desc: Repurpose a completed YouTube video into newsletter issues, social posts, and other formats.

skills.name: community-post
skills.desc: Create YouTube community posts that drive views and audience engagement.

skills.name: ideate-notes
skills.desc: Generate Substack Note ideas by scanning published YouTube videos, newsletters, and Notes.

skills.name: write-note
skills.desc: Create high-engagement Substack Notes. Single short posts or promotion of a newsletter issue.

skills.name: youtube-data
skills.desc: Retrieve YouTube data via the YouTube Data API — video search, channel details, transcripts, comments, trends. Requires YOUTUBE_API_KEY and uv.

agents.name: principal-engineer

agents.name: youtube-researcher
agents.desc: YouTube research specialist. Uses the YouTube Data API to analyze channels, videos, comments, transcripts, and related content.

prompts.name: wrap
prompts.desc: End-of-session summary and publish. Saves local handoff file via agent-session-summary, then publishes HTML artifact to pna-sessions.vercel.app and notifies via Telegram.
